content.problemStatement:
There is a street with plots on both sides. Each side has NNN plots numbered from 1 to N1~to~N1 to N. You need to find the number of ways to place houses on these plots such that no two houses are adjacent on the same side of the street. A plot can either be empty or have a house. The same plot number on opposite sides of the street can both have houses.


Input Format

First line contains TTT, the number of test cases
Next TTT line contains a single integer NNN


Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer representing the number of possible arrangements modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:
All plots empty 
House on left side only 
House on right side only 
Houses on both sides
Test Case 2: With 2 plots on each side, there are 9 possible arrangements:
All empty 
One house on any plot (4 possibilities) 
Two houses on opposite sides of street (4 possibilities)
